steps: Write the first equation in slope-intercept form: $15x + y = 13$ gives $y = -15x + 13$.
Write the second equation in slope-intercept form: $30x + 2y = 26$ → divide every term by 2 → $15x + y = 13$ → $y = -15x + 13$. The equations are identical.

Test all four choices:
  • Choice A — 'Exactly one': A unique solution requires two lines with different slopes that intersect at one point. Both lines here have slope $-15$ and the same intercept 13 — they are the same line. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Exactly two': Linear systems never have exactly two solutions by the nature of lines. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Infinitely many': Since the second equation is exactly 2 times the first, both equations represent the same line. Every point on that line is a solution. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Zero': Zero solutions requires parallel lines with different intercepts (same slope, different y-intercept). Here the intercepts are equal, so they are not distinct parallel lines. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
desmosExpressions: y = 13 - 15x shows both lines overlap completely.
